post_idea: A 'Tonight's Pick' featuring the Paper Plane cocktail as a sophisticated yet easy-to-make weeknight drink
caption: Tonight's pick: the Paper Plane. Equal parts bourbon, Aperol, Amaro Nonino & fresh lemon. Perfectly balanced, endlessly impressive. 🛩️ Save it in Spirit Library and make it yours.
image_prompt: A beautifully lit coupe glass containing a golden-amber Paper Plane cocktail, placed on a dark walnut bar surface. Soft, warm side lighting creates a cinematic glow through the drink. A single lemon twist garnish rests on the rim. In the soft-focus background, a bottle of bourbon and a bottle of Amaro Nonino are barely visible. The mood is intimate and elevated, like a quiet cocktail bar at golden hour. Shot in a modern editorial photography style with shallow depth of field, rich warm tones, and minimal composition. No text or logos in the image.
reasoning: The Paper Plane is a modern classic that appeals to both seasoned cocktail enthusiasts and curious home bartenders — it's equal-parts simple yet uses an ingredient (Amaro Nonino) that makes people feel like they're leveling up. A 'Tonight's Pick' format performs well for the brand and creates urgency. Mid-week is perfect timing for inspiring someone to shake something special at home. This covers the recipes and techniques pillar while also subtly educating on a lesser-known amaro, and the CTA drives back to the app naturally.

post_idea: Spotlight the Penicillin cocktail as a smoky, honeyed modern classic that deserves a place in every home bartender's repertoire.
caption:
The Penicillin. Blended scotch, fresh lemon, honey-ginger syrup — then a float of smoky Islay single malt that changes everything. Modern classic status, fully earned. 🥃

post_idea: Spotlight the Last Word cocktail as a perfectly balanced, mysterious Prohibition-era classic that punches above its weight.
caption:
The Last Word. Equal parts gin, green Chartreuse, maraschino, and lime — a Prohibition-era knockout that still wins every argument.

Comment below your favorite recipes!

post_idea: Spotlight the Aviation cocktail as a floral, violet-hued gin classic that feels like drinking a sunset at 30,000 feet.
caption:
The Aviation. Gin, maraschino, crème de violette, fresh lemon — shaken until the glass turns the colour of a fading sky. Floral, tart, and utterly elegant. This is the cocktail that makes people ask, "what are you drinking?" ✈️

post_idea: Spotlight the 20th Century cocktail as a forgotten Art Deco gem combining gin, chocolate, and lemon in unexpected harmony.
caption:
The 20th Century. Gin, crème de cacao, Lillet Blanc, and fresh lemon — a cocktail that sounds impossible on paper but tastes like velvet in a glass. Chocolate and citrus have no business working this well together, and yet here we are. 🍋✨

In the background, slightly out of focus: a crystal mixing glass and a bottle of Lillet Blanc. The mood is Art Deco sophistication meets modern minimalism. Shot in a cinematic style with shallow depth of field, rich tones, and editorial cocktail photography quality. No text or logos.
reasoning: The 20th Century is listed in the app but hasn't been featured recently. It's a perfect pick because it's lesser-known enough to feel educational and discoverable, yet its flavor profile — chocolate, citrus, floral — is genuinely surprising and conversation-starting. It contrasts nicely with the recent run of spirit-forward and herbal cocktails (Penicillin, Last Word, Aviation, Paper Plane), offering something lighter and more unexpected. The name itself evokes elegance and history, which aligns with the brand's sophisticated-but-approachable tone.

post_idea: Spotlight the Negroni as the ultimate three-ingredient masterclass in bitter, sweet, and boozy balance.
caption:
The Negroni. Three ingredients. Equal parts. Zero room for error — and that's exactly what makes it perfect. Gin, Campari, and sweet vermouth in a conversation so balanced, not a single voice dominates. Bitter, bright, and utterly unforgettable. 🍊

post_idea: Spotlight the Mai Tai as a rum-forward tiki icon that's far more complex and elegant than its reputation suggests.
caption:
The Mai Tai. Not the neon slushy you had on vacation — the real one. Aged rum, orgeat, curaçao, and fresh lime shaken over crushed ice. It's nutty, citrusy, and deeply layered. One sip and you'll understand why Trader Vic called it the finest drink in the world 🌺

Shallow depth of field, rich tones, editorial cocktail photography style, shot on medium format camera, natural textures, no text or logos.
reasoning: The Mai Tai hasn't been featured in recent posts and offers a great contrast to the string of spirit-forward and gin-based classics (Negroni, Aviation, Last Word, 20th Century). It's one of the most misunderstood cocktails — people associate it with frozen beach drinks — so there's a strong educational angle in revealing the original recipe's sophistication. It also taps into summer energy and tiki culture, which performs well visually on Instagram. The slightly provocative 'not the neon slushy' framing creates a hook that stops the scroll and invites engagement from cocktail enthusiasts who know the difference.

post_idea: Spotlight the Daiquiri as the deceptively simple, impossibly elegant rum classic that separates great bartenders from good ones.
caption:
The Daiquiri. Three ingredients. Zero room to hide. White rum, fresh lime, sugar — shaken until frost climbs the tin. It's the drink bartenders order to test other bartenders. When it's right, it's pure liquid clarity. 🧊

post_idea: Spotlight the Paloma cocktail as a sun-drenched, effortlessly refreshing tequila classic that rivals (and maybe outshines) the Margarita.
caption:
The Paloma. Tequila, grapefruit, lime, and a long pour of sparkling soda over ice. It's the drink all of Mexico reaches for — bright, bittersweet, and completely effortless. One sip and you'll wonder why you ever defaulted to a Margarita. 🌸

post_idea: Spotlight the Espresso Martini as the bold, velvety collision of coffee and cocktail culture that became the modern classic of a generation.
caption:
The Espresso Martini. Vodka, coffee liqueur, fresh espresso, and a whisper of simple syrup — shaken hard until it's ice-cold and crowned with a perfect crema. Bold, bittersweet, and impossibly smooth. This is the drink that turned every dinner party into an afterparty ☕🍸
